title: Uncut Gems
logline: A charismatic New York City jeweler and compulsive gambler risks everything—his family, his business, and his life—when he bets it all on an Ethiopian opal and NBA star Amare Stoudemire in a desperate attempt to pay off dangerous debts.
antagonistOrConflict: Howard's gambling addiction and the violent loan sharks (Jim, Robert, Tuna) he owes money to
tone: Frenetic, anxiety-inducing, darkly comedic, relentlessly propulsive
themes: The American Dream corrupted by greed
Debt as a form of violence

actBreaks.description: Howard meets with loan shark Anthony at Boratta's Restaurant, establishing the full scope of his debt crisis and the violent consequences he faces.

validation.ruling: Scene #32 marks an irreversible turning point where Howard places a massive 5-team parlay bet ($65k at 4-to-1 odds) with Anthony, fundamentally shifting the story from establishing Howard's chaotic world to the high-stakes gambling scheme that will drive Act 2.

actBreaks.description: The auction at Christie's fails catastrophically when Amare doesn't show up, forcing Howard to retrieve the gem and face the collapse of his escape plan.

validation.ruling: Scene #84 delivers a devastating reversal when expert Aren reveals the black opal is not the valuable harlequin multi-color precious stone Howard believed, but flawed and worth far less—destroying Howard's auction plan and forcing him into the desperate final act.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script operates like a pressure cooker with a faulty release valve. The structure is deceptively classical—three acts, clear turning points—but the pacing strategy is pure chaos theory. Rather than building tension through traditional escalation, the Safdies maintain a constant state of crisis from page 3 onward, with Howard perpetually one step ahead of disaster and two steps behind solvency. The opening prologue (scenes 1-2) in the Ethiopian mine functions as both origin story for the MacGuffin and visual metaphor for the entire narrative: something precious extracted through violence and chaos. The script then immediately plunges into Howard's world already in motion, already in debt, already lying. Act breaks don't provide relief or reflection—they're simply new configurations of the same fundamental problem. The auction failure at scene 84 doesn't introduce a new obstacle; it removes Howard's last legitimate exit strategy, forcing him into the locked-door climax that's been inevitable since he first saw that gem.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is relentless and deliberately exhausting. Scenes average 1.5 pages but feel even shorter due to constant interruptions, overlapping dialogue, and Howard's inability to finish one crisis before starting another. The script uses geographic and temporal fragmentation (Ethiopia to Queens to Miami to Vegas to New York in the first 21 scenes) to establish Howard's scattered, manic energy. The middle section (scenes 25-82) creates a false sense of spatial stability—most action confined to the jewelry office—while actually accelerating the chaos through rapid-fire entrances, exits, and reversals. The final act (scenes 98-119) compresses time brutally: the auction, the confrontation with Jim, the lockdown, and the game all happen in what feels like real-time, trapping the audience inside Howard's final gamble with no escape.

incitingIncident.description: Howard arrives at Nadav's ransacked apartment and deduces that Arthur, Nadav's black friend from the gym who smoked by the open window, likely robbed him; Howard tells Nadav to pack a bag and come stay with him, then calls a car service and instructs Nadav to go to LaGuardia and fly to Miami.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: INT. NADAV’S APARTMENT, SUNNYSIDE QUEENS - AFTER

validation.ruling: Scene #9 shatters Nadav's ordinary world when Howard reveals that Arthur (his new friend) orchestrated the robbery by leaving the window open, establishing the central conflict of trust, deception, and Howard's dangerous world that will drive the narrative forward.

midpoint.description: Howard climbs into the limo where Jim demands 250 grand by Wednesday to settle the debt, while Robert punches Howard in the chest and shaves off a chunk of his hair with an electric razor.

validation.ruling: Scene #64 is a definitive point of no return where Jim gives Howard a 48-hour ultimatum for $250k or face lethal consequences, escalating stakes from financial to life-threatening and forcing Howard from reactive scrambling to proactive desperation in his gambling scheme.

climax.description: Jim, Robert, and Nico sit trapped in the vestibule while Howard sets up a TV so they can watch; Nadav calls confirming the bet was placed, and the game begins with Amare scoring an immediate alley-oop.
climax.sceneHeading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE- SHOWROOM - SAME TIME

validation.ruling: Scene #114 shows Howard trapping Jim in the vestibule but is setup/confrontation buildup, not the climax itself. Scene #119 (p114, 6pp, final scene) is the actual climax where the parlay bet resolves at Foxwoods simultaneous with the showroom confrontation reaching its peak—this is where the main story question is answered and maximum tension/stakes converge in the script's final pages.

scenes.heading: EXT. OPAL MINE - ETHIOPIA - DAY
scenes.summary: White foremen violently argue with local black miners at an Ethiopian opal mine, escalating into a riot as two black men use the chaos to sneak inside the mine.

scenes.heading: INT. OPAL MINE - ETHIOPIA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Two men descend into the mine with headlamps, retrieve a hidden t-shirt from a crevice, extract a grapefruit-sized rock from the dirt, grind windows into it, and inspect it under light as twinkling electronic music plays.

scenes.heading: INT. UNIVERSAL BOXING GYM, QUEENS, NY - RING -NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nadav boxes a young Latino fighter at Universal Boxing Gym and is immediately hit three times in the head while Arthur yells excitedly from ringside.

scenes.heading: INT. UNIVERSAL BOXING GYM, QUEENS, NY - LOCKERROOM - AFTER
scenes.summary: Nadav changes naked in the locker room while Arthur invites him to Glazz nightclub; Nadav agrees and says he needs to go home first.

scenes.heading: INT. NADAV’S APARTMENT, SUNNYSIDE QUEENS - BEDROOM - AFTER
scenes.summary: Nadav applies gel and cologne in his bedroom, selects a gold chain with a Star of David pendant and a gold Rolex from his underwear drawer, while Arthur smokes by the window.

scenes.heading: INT. GLAZZ NIGHT CLUB - AFTER
scenes.summary: Arthur and Dev flirt with girls at the bar in Glazz nightclub while Nadav, one of few white people present, clings to them.

scenes.heading: INT. NADAV’S APARTMENT, BUILDING HALLWAY - 12AM
scenes.summary: Nadav, drunk, walks down his hallway at midnight and unlocks his apartment door.

scenes.heading: INT. NADAV’S APARTMENT - AFTER
scenes.summary: Nadav discovers his apartment ransacked with bed overturned, drawers emptied, mirror broken, TV and stereo missing, his mother's jewelry and chains stolen; he bursts into tears and frantically calls Howard begging him to pick up.

scenes.heading: INT. NADAV’S APARTMENT, SUNNYSIDE QUEENS - AFTER
scenes.summary: Howard arrives at Nadav's ransacked apartment and deduces that Arthur, Nadav's black friend from the gym who smoked by the open window, likely robbed him; Howard tells Nadav to pack a bag and come stay with him, then calls a car service and instructs Nadav to go to LaGuardia and fly to Miami.
scenes.turningPointNote: Howard is introduced as the protagonist dealing with a crisis involving his nephew; establishes his problem-solving approach and hints at his own troubles (needing to leave town).

scenes.heading: EXT. LAGUARDIA AIRPORT - 2 AM
scenes.summary: Howard arrives at LaGuardia Airport at 2 AM, pays the cab driver with bills from a Duane Reade bag, and rushes into the terminal.

scenes.heading: INT. MIAMI HIGH RISE APARTMENT BUILDING - 6 AM
scenes.summary: Howard rings the doorbell at a Miami high-rise apartment at 6 AM and is greeted by his enraged sister-in-law Carmelle who grabs him by the wrist and jerks him inside.

scenes.heading: INT. GABI NAGY’S APARTMENT, LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Carmelle shows Howard the empty living room with only folding chairs and a card table, shoves a printed note about a $17,000 gold sofa set in his face, and Howard ignores her to rush into the bathroom.

scenes.heading: INT. GABI NAGY’S APARTMENT, BATHROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard locks himself in the steam-filled bathroom where his naked brother Gabi is showering; Howard tries to apologize and explain his problems while Gabi furiously tells him to get out, steps out of the shower, and threatens to call the police if Howard doesn't leave immediately.

scenes.heading: EXT. MIAMI STREET, NEAR BEACH. 7 AM
scenes.summary: Howard walks a Miami street at 7 AM smoking and talking on the phone with Jim, apologizing for his debt and promising to deposit 175 grand into accounts before the bank manager arrives, while Jim lectures him about trust and consequences.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH, SOUTH BEACH MIAMI - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard puts his phone on speaker to check an online sports betting site while Jim continues lecturing him about being in a bad spot and warning that his partners aren't as nice; Howard zooms in on betting spreads while barely listening.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPLANE. DAYTIME, 30,000 FEET.
scenes.summary: Howard sits in first class drinking a Coke Zero and compulsively rips pages of luxury items from a SkyMall Magazine, putting them in his pocket.

scenes.heading: INT. MIRAGE CASINO, LAS VEAGAS. CHIP EXCHANGE - DAYTIME
scenes.summary: Howard exchanges cash from the Duane Reade bag for $500 chips at the Mirage Casino, then gambles at craps, roulette, and poker tables, making money hand over fist while looking manic.

scenes.heading: INT. MIRAGE CASINO, LAS VEAGAS. SPORTS BOOK - AFTER
scenes.summary: Howard places a $60,000 parlay bet on multiple NBA games and a $40,000 parlay on college football games at the Mirage sports book.

scenes.heading: INT. MIRAGE CASINO, LAS VEGAS. NEAR SPORTS BOOK.
scenes.summary: Howard plays blackjack alone buying all seven hands with $1,000 chips while watching ESPN on distant TV screens, splits pairs, doubles down, hits blackjack on one hand, and tips the waitress a $100 chip.

scenes.heading: INT. BELLAGIO HOTEL, LAS VEGAS. PRESIDENTIAL SUITE. EVENING.
scenes.summary: Howard sits with boxer Ray Forcaster in his presidential suite inspecting bedazzled jewelry from a briefcase, retrieves a money sign pinky ring from Ray's bodyguard, discusses a jewel-encrusted Rolls Royce, tells Ray that Jews and Blacks are the same people, and Ray reads a message on his phone saying his wife is downstairs.

scenes.heading: EXT. TETERBOROUGH PRIVATE AIRPORT - ARRIVALS - EARLY MORNING.
scenes.summary: Howard exits the private airport carrying Ray's two young children, double-kisses Ray's wife goodbye, and gets into Yussi's white BMW asking about water bottles.

scenes.heading: EXT. DIAMOND DISTRICT - 46TH STREET BTW 6TH AND 5TH - 11 AM
scenes.summary: Howard and Yussi jaywalk across 46th Street into an arcade throughway, with Howard carrying flowers and a shopping bag.

scenes.heading: INT. 47TH STREET JEWELRY ARCADE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard and Yussi cut through a congested jewelry arcade filled with glass showcases and dealers; Howard stops at Joshua's booth and tells him he's getting crusty.

scenes.heading: EXT. 47TH ST BETWEEN 6TH & 5TH AVE, THE DIAMOND DISTRICT - AFTER
scenes.summary: A street peddler runs up to Howard and Yussi on 47th Street showing a diamond-encrusted Hublot watch; Howard glances at it, declines the offer, and they turn into a building.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - SHOWROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Sadie helps a white customer select diamonds for an engagement ring while Larry confronts Howard about unpaid debts; NBA player Amare Stoudemire's bodyguard arrives and Howard asks Larry to leave, then buzzes in Amare, Demany, and the bodyguards while Yussi goes to get something from the back.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE- BACKROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard finds Dina on the phone in the backroom gathering her things; he shoves a handful of cash into her jacket pocket telling her to get something nice at Barney's and have lunch at Fred's.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE- SHOWROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dina leaves while Larry demands payment; Howard claims he hasn't touched over 5 grand in a month and was in the hospital, but Larry says he checked both hospitals Howard mentioned; Amare's bodyguard enters and Howard asks Larry to leave, then Amare enters with his entourage and Howard shows him jewelry while Demany asks about the Ethiopian opal.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - BACKROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard opens a crate containing vacuum-sealed fish, slices them open searching until he finds a wet black plastic bag containing a large uncut 600-carat black opal from the African mine.
scenes.turningPointNote: The central MacGuffin of the film—the Ethiopian black opal—is revealed and retrieved, setting the main plot in motion.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - SHOWROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard rushes out with the black opal and presents it to Amare, who leans on the glass showcase to inspect it closely through a loupe, becoming transfixed by the gem.

scenes.heading: INT. INSIDE THE GEMSTONE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The camera zooms into the gemstone revealing a kaleidoscope of color, light, and swirling abstract shapes in a hauntingly beautiful psychedelic trip.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - SHOWROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Amare's elbows crash through the glass showcase covering his arms in blood; everyone screams and jumps back while Sadie tends to his wounds; Amare, dazed, asks to take the gem for one night promising Instagram posts, and Howard reluctantly agrees after Amare offers to tag him and post about the stone.
scenes.turningPointNote: Howard makes the catastrophic decision to lend out the opal to Amare despite it being set for auction, establishing the central conflict that drives the rest of the film.

scenes.heading: INT. BORATTA’S RESTAURANT - EARLY EVENING
scenes.summary: Howard meets Anthony at Boratta's restaurant offering $65,000 cash and a Rolex watch to place a five-team parlay bet; Anthony is reluctant but Howard insists it's not a credit line and eventually Anthony agrees to take the action.

scenes.heading: INT. BAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Howard sits in a sports bar watching the Knicks game and checking his Instagram, discovering he has 549 new followers from a photo with Amare; he watches Amare's mythical performance on TV and claps enthusiastically.

scenes.heading: EXT. 3RD AVENUE. AFTER.
scenes.summary: Howard walks down the street listening to a game on his phone; when the broadcaster says the team will dribble out the clock, Howard stops and urges Eastrick to go for the triple-double, celebrating when Eastrick calls a timeout and gets his tenth assist.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST 55TH STREET - AFTER
scenes.summary: Howard approaches a sleeping homeless man, places a container of steak next to him and shoves hundred-dollar bills into his coat pocket; the homeless man suddenly springs up revealing a gaunt grizzled face, causing Howard to scream and scurry away.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD’S APARMENT BUILDING - JUST AFTER
scenes.summary: Howard enters his apartment building where the doorman greets him; he asks the concierge if anyone came by and instructs him to say they left town if anyone does.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD’S APARTMENT- KITCHEN/LIVING ROOM - AFTER
scenes.summary: Howard enters his garishly decorated apartment calling for Sadie, announcing he brought dinner, and walks through the living room littered with infomercial gadgets toward the bedroom.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD’S APARTMENT. THE BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard walks through his messy bedroom with clothes spilling from the closet and an unmade bed, calling out for Sadie before entering the bathroom.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD’S APARTMENT- BATHROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard finds Sadie in the bathtub and tells her he brought steak from Wollensky's; she covers herself and says she'll eat later; Howard asks about smudge marks on the mirror and suggests getting a maid more often, then Sadie tells him she's moving out.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD’S APARTMENT- HOWARD’S BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard examines fingerprint-like smudge marks on the bedroom mirror while unbuttoning his shirt, then turns on the 70-inch television and leaves the room.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD’S APARTMENT- KITCHEN/LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard powers up his six flat screens one at a time, unmutes Amare's giddy post-game interview where he talks about gods beckoning from above, and Howard tells the TV he loves Amare before walking back to his bedroom.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD’S APARTMENT- HOWARD’S BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard leaves a voicemail for Demany insisting he needs the gem back by 9 AM, then closes the closet door exposing topless Sadie; she protests and he argues about buying her things and bringing her food while she tells him the place is a mess and she's moving out.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE- BACKROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Howard sits at his desk at 10:27 AM waiting for Demany to answer the phone; the call goes to voicemail with Biggie's 'Gimme One More Chance' and Howard leaves an angry message demanding Demany answer his phone.

scenes.heading: EXT. MIDTOWN STREET - DIAMOND DISTRICT- AFTER
scenes.summary: Larry confronts Howard on the street about the $80,000 debt, mentioning he heard about Howard's $65,000 parlay; Howard unbuckles his Rolex watch and hands it to Larry as partial payment before rushing away.

scenes.heading: EXT. BED-STUY PROJECTS - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Howard walks past young black males shooting dice in the Bed-Stuy projects and enters a building.

scenes.heading: INT. DEMANY’S APARTMENT
scenes.summary: Demany opens his door looking hungover; Howard angrily confronts him about missing the 9 AM meeting; Demany says he just woke up after being at the club until 5 AM and reveals Amare still has the gem.

scenes.heading: INT. DEMANY’S LEXUS - DAY
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. KNICKS PRACTICE FACILITY - AFTER
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: EXT. CITY STREET - AFTER
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. LACE STRIP CLUB - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: EXT. SCHOOL BUILDING, QUEENS, NY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Howard moves through a crowd and enters a school building at night.

scenes.heading: INT. SCHOOL AUDITORIUM - AFTER
scenes.summary: Howard sits with Dina and his sons Eddie and Beni in the school auditorium waiting for the play to start, joking with Eddie by tapping a stranger's shoulder.

scenes.heading: INT. SCHOOL AUDITORIUM - 10 ROWS BACK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard confronts Tuna and Robert who are sitting in the auditorium, realizes they're there to intimidate him, and quickly walks toward the exits.

scenes.heading: INT. SCHOOL HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard calls Jim while walking down the hallway to complain about the intimidation at his daughter's school, then sees Robert and Tuna appear and walks in the opposite direction.

scenes.heading: INT. ANOTHER SCHOOL HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard picks up his pace, tries locked doors, then finds an open door and enters just as Robert and Tuna turn the corner.

scenes.heading: INT. SCHOOL AUDITORIUM - BACK STAGE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard crosses through backstage where costumed children wait, briefly greets his daughter Marcel who is in the play, then exits through doors on the opposite side.

scenes.heading: INT. SCHOOL STAIRWELL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard rushes down stairs to the basement and through dark corridors until he reaches the boiler room.

scenes.heading: INT. SCHOOL BOILER ROOM - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Howard hides behind the hot boiler and burns his arm, holds his breath as Robert and Tuna search the room, then hears Robert burn his hand on the boiler before they decide to wait outside.

scenes.heading: INT. SCHOOL AUDITORIUM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marcel performs as Snow White on stage, accepting the poisoned apple from the Queen, while Dina checks her phone for messages from Howard.

scenes.heading: INT. SCHOOL BOILER ROOM - MUCH LATER
scenes.summary: Howard, covered in soot and grease, slips out from behind the boiler and uses his cellphone to light his way through the room.

scenes.heading: INT. SCHOOL HALLWAY - AFTER
scenes.summary: Howard finds the hallway exit doors locked, his phone dies leaving him in darkness, and he tries locked classroom doors before returning to the basement.

scenes.heading: INT. SCHOOL BASEMENT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard pushes on basement windows until he finds a loose one, pulls boxes over, and climbs through it barely fitting.

scenes.heading: EXT. SCHOOL PLAYGROUND - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard emerges in the playground, finds the gate locked, scales the fence ripping his pants, then gets hit in the back with a baseball bat by Jim's guy as a limo pulls up.

scenes.heading: INT. LIMOUSINE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard climbs into the limo where Jim demands 250 grand by Wednesday to settle the debt, while Robert punches Howard in the chest and shaves off a chunk of his hair with an electric razor.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jim gives Howard an ultimatum with a hard deadline of Wednesday for $250k, escalating the central conflict and establishing the ticking clock that will drive the rest of the film.

scenes.heading: EXT. GREENHOUSE NIGHT CLUB - AFTER THE CAR RIDE
scenes.summary: Howard, disheveled and missing hair, arrives at Greenhouse nightclub with Jim and Michael, bribes the bouncer saying Amare is expecting them, and they enter.

scenes.heading: INT. GREENHOUSE NIGHT CLUB - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard and Jim navigate through the packed nightclub until Howard spots one of Amare's bodyguards at a banquette in the back.

scenes.heading: INT. GREENHOUSE NIGHT CLUB - BANQUETTE
scenes.summary: Howard approaches Amare's associates Kev and Paris at the banquette, asking for Privelej and Demany while introducing Jim, but Jim is clearly not entertained.

scenes.heading: INT. GREENHOUSE NIGHT CLUB - BATHROOM HALLWAY
scenes.summary: Howard bangs on the bathroom door looking for Sadie, gets pushed against the wall by two big men, then Privelej emerges from the bathroom looking wasted with unbuckled pants.

scenes.heading: EXT. GREENHOUSE NIGHT CLUB - BACK ALLEY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard is thrown out into the back alley, pounds on the locked doors screaming threats, then Demany opens the door to hand over Sadie before Howard has a brief breakdown.

scenes.heading: INT. CAB - AFTER
scenes.summary: Howard stares out the cab window in silent rage while Sadie repeatedly tries to turn off the taxi TV until Howard swats her hand away and does it himself.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOWARD’S BUILDING
scenes.summary: The cab pulls up to Howard's building where a doorman helps Sadie out while Howard stays inside and tells the driver to continue.

scenes.heading: EXT. DIAMOND DISTRICT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Howard walks down the deserted 47th street in the Diamond District at night.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - BACKROOM - 145AM
scenes.summary: Howard enters his office, turns on the lights, throws his jacket on the desk, and sets up a makeshift bed on the floor with a blanket and pillow.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE- BACKROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Howard talks on the phone about the opal while holding a tape dispenser as a stand-in, discussing auction details and imagery, when Yussi arrives on the security monitor causing Howard to hang up.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE- SHOWROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Yussi exits through the showroom, and Howard yells that Yussi is a big boy as Yussi slams both security doors on his way out.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE- BACKROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard continues mocking Yussi, then takes Sadie's call on speakerphone where she says she feels horrible, but Howard sarcastically tells her to rest while he works, then hangs up when another call comes in.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - SHOWROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Joani buzzes Amare and his bodyguards in but the interior door won't open due to a bent magnetic release, trapping them in the vestibule while Howard inspects the lock and Amare shows the gem through the glass.

scenes.heading: EXT. 47TH STREET BETWEEN 6TH AND 5TH, DIAMOND DISTRICT - AFTER
scenes.summary: Elan rushes down the block with a sledgehammer, arrives at Howard's building confused, then hears Howard and others yelling from above and looks up to see them.

scenes.heading: EXT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - OUT WINDOW - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard has someone dial Elan's cell, instructs him to tie the hammer to the twine, then raises it back up but the knot comes loose halfway and the hammer falls to the ground.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - SHOWROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Howard apologizes to the sweating Amare and offers 30% off, then repeatedly whacks the door with the sledgehammer while Nadav takes over, until Howard uses metal shavings and a screwdriver to finally open the door.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE- BACKROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Amare tells Howard the stone is spiritually important to him and offers $175,000 cash plus courtside seats for the season, but Howard says it's worth more and is going to auction, then offers to let Amare keep it until Wednesday for collateral.
scenes.turningPointNote: Howard makes the critical decision to loan the opal to Amare as collateral, setting up the central gamble that will drive the climax—this deal directly connects to his Wednesday deadline with Jim.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE- SHOWROOM
scenes.summary: Howard rushes through the showroom telling Joani he's going to Aren's at GIA and to close up without him.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE- VESTIBULE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard opens the door to find Sadie with a puffy face, waves a finger in her face saying no, then rushes to the elevator while she runs after him apologizing and begging.

scenes.heading: INT. CHRISTIES AUCTION HOUSE BACK ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Aren examines the opal and tells Howard it's not the harlequin multi-color black precious he claimed, pointing out it has basic pin-fire pattern, limited color, and won't face up properly, estimating it at $30-40k instead of Howard's $300k valuation.
scenes.turningPointNote: The opal's true value is revealed to be a fraction of what Howard claimed, undermining his entire plan to pay off Jim and creating a major crisis in his scheme.

scenes.heading: INT. GOOEY’S - PARK AVENUE APARTMENT - DINING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Howard sits at Gooey's formal Shabbos dinner table with both families when his phone rings, he silences it, then notices and asks about a new painting above the fireplace.

scenes.heading: INT. GOOEY’S - PARK AVENUE APARTMENT - HALLWAY TO DINING ROOM
scenes.summary: Howard steps into the hallway and answers Sadie's call, angrily asking why she's calling him during Shabbos dinner.

scenes.heading: INT. GOOEY’S - PARK AVENUE APARTMENT - CHILDRENS BEDROOM
scenes.summary: Howard enters a lavishly decorated children's bedroom while on the phone with Sadie, who is crying and asking to talk, but Howard tells her he's having dinner and will enjoy a peaceful weekend away from her.

scenes.heading: INT. GOOEY’S - PARK AVENUE APARTMENT - DEN - DURING
scenes.summary: Howard sits in Gooey's den watching ESPN show Amare's terrible performance and erratic behavior, then tells Gooey that Amare tried to buy the opal for $175k but Howard refused because it's going to auction.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD AND DINA’S FAMILY CAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Howard drives his family's BMW with Dina and the kids, checks the time showing 10:15, and tells Dina he needs to grab something from the apartment when she questions his route.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOWARD’S BUILDING - AFTER
scenes.summary: Howard pulls up to his building, tells the doorman he's just popping up for a second, and enters the lobby while Dina and the children watch.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD’S APARTMENT. LIVING ROOM/KITCHENETTE - AFTER
scenes.summary: Howard bursts into the apartment raging at Sadie for relaxing at 10:30, rummages through her handbag to take her house keys, tells her the next time she closes the door will be the last, slams it, then immediately returns to grab his phone charger.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD’S APARTMENT - BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard throws open the closet, grabs a suitcase, and violently stuffs it with armfuls of Sadie's clothing, shoes, and drawer contents while ranting about her sexual behavior.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD’S APARTMENT - LIVING ROOM/KITCHENETTE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard rolls the suitcase to the front door threatening to have it thrown in the garbage if Sadie doesn't come down with two more suitcases within the hour, then slams the door when she asks if he's coming back.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD’S APARTMENT - BUILDING HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard rolls the suitcase to the elevator, then opens the stairwell door and hides the suitcase behind a trash can.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD AND DINA’S FAMILY CAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Howard gets back in the car, Dina icily asks if he got what he wanted, he says yes, then silently starts the engine and drives away.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET IN SUBURBAN QUEENS, FOREST HILLS - AFTER
scenes.summary: The BMW drives down a tree-lined street of oversized homes in suburban Queens and pulls into a driveway.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD AND DINA’S FAMILY CAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Nagy family exits the car, Dina tells Howard to pull out the recycling, and Howard slowly drags the bin to the curb as lights turn on inside before shuffling up the stairs and entering his home.

scenes.heading: INT. CHRISTIE’S AUCTION HOUSE - AUCTION FLOOR - AFTER
scenes.summary: At the Christie's auction, the opal appears on screen but the auctioneer reads a formal apology stating the item description was inaccurate, then Gooey bids it up to $190k against Amare's assistant while Howard watches from the side and Jim and Robert watch from the back.
scenes.turningPointNote: The opal sells for $190k to Gooey in a shill bidding scheme, creating the illusion of success but setting up Howard's exposure and the unraveling of his plan.

scenes.heading: INT. CHRISTIE’S AUCTION HOUSE - HALLWAY - AFTER
scenes.summary: Gooey confronts Howard in the hallway about the low $190k price, Howard admits he was wrong and promises to wire the money back once it clears plus Christie's 12% from his own pocket, then Lloyd stops them.

scenes.heading: INT. CHRISTIE’S AUCTION HOUSE - LLOYD’S OFFICE
scenes.summary: Lloyd tells Howard and Gooey they are both banned from Christie's for shill bidding, Howard and Gooey protest claiming a friend can buy through auction, and Howard demands proof.

scenes.heading: EXT. CHRISTIE’S AUCTION HOUSE - 49TH ST - AFTER
scenes.summary: Howard and Gooey leave Christie's with the gem, but Jim and Robert immediately confront them; Howard lies that the auction bid was disqualified and doesn't count, while Jim refuses to hear his excuses.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD’S APARTMENT - BATHROOM - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Howard showers while Sadie enters naked and he rejects her advances; she climbs on the toilet with a shampoo bottle and waits for him to close his eyes.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD’S APARTMENT - BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard runs dripping wet to answer a phone call from Amare, expressing relief and joy while Sadie enters the bedroom and lies down next to him awaiting eye contact.

scenes.heading: EXT. 47TH STREET BETWEEN 6TH & 5TH, DIAMOND DISTRICT - NEXT DAY
scenes.summary: Howard approaches Jim's parked Mercedes on 47th Street and tells them Amare will arrive by 4pm; Jim reveals his friend Nico is already waiting upstairs.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - BACKROOM - AFTER
scenes.summary: Amare arrives with a bag of cash to buy the gem; Howard counts the money, gives Amare the opal, and Amare leaves for medical while Nico watches from the showroom with the door kept open.
scenes.turningPointNote: Howard receives the cash payment from Amare and hands over the gem, completing the sale that sets up the final bet.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - SHOWROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Nadav tells Sadie he's going to the gym and leaves the office.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - BACKROOM
scenes.summary: Amare hugs Howard goodbye and exits the backroom to head to medical.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - SHOWROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Amare kisses Sadie goodbye, asks about Joani, then Jim interrupts and gushes over Amare while Howard watches on the monitor.

scenes.heading: EXT. 43RD AND 5TH AVENUE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Nadav gets into a Lincoln Town Car parked on the corner.

scenes.heading: INT. LINCOLN TOWN CAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Nadav offers the driver $400 to take him to Foxwoods Casino in Connecticut via the fastest route.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - BACKROOM - AFTER
scenes.summary: Howard reveals to Jim and Robert that he sent Nadav to Foxwoods with Amare's cash to place a parlay bet with 4-to-1 odds, offering them $400k but they reject it.
scenes.turningPointNote: Howard reveals he's gambled all of Amare's money instead of paying his debt, triggering the violent confrontation that follows.

scenes.heading: EXT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - BACKROOM - OUT OF WINDOW
scenes.summary: Robert and Nico dangle Howard out the window while he screams for help; Jim demands he call Nadav and Howard agrees after being threatened further.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE- BACKROOM
scenes.summary: Howard sits at his desk trembling while Jim orders him to dial Nadav's number on speakerphone, but after a long pause Howard refuses, saying he can't do it.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - SHOWROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard traps Jim, Robert, and Nico in the security vestibule by having Sadie refuse to buzz them out, then yells through the glass defending his bet while they rage and threaten him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Howard locks the loan sharks in the vestibule, fully committing to the bet and crossing the point of no return with Jim.

scenes.heading: INT. LINCOLN TOWN CAR - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Nadav sits in bumper-to-bumper traffic at 5:45pm and offers the driver an extra $200 to arrive before 7pm, but the GPS shows 7:35pm arrival.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOXWOODS CASINO, LEDYARD, CT. - 6:55PM.
scenes.summary: The Lincoln pulls up to Foxwoods at 6:55pm; Nadav jumps out, slips and falls, then gets up and runs into the casino.

scenes.heading: INT. FOXWOODS CASINO, LEDYARD, CT. - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Nadav asks a stranger for directions to the sports betting area inside the casino.

scenes.heading: INT. FOXWOODS CASINO, LEDYARD, CT. - SPORTS BOOK
scenes.summary: Nadav reaches the sports book and places a $175,000 two-way parlay bet with a female teller who calls over a supervisor due to the large amount.
scenes.turningPointNote: The massive bet is successfully placed just before game time, locking in Howard's all-or-nothing gamble.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE- SHOWROOM - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Jim, Robert, and Nico sit trapped in the vestibule while Howard sets up a TV so they can watch; Nadav calls confirming the bet was placed, and the game begins with Amare scoring an immediate alley-oop.
